comment.body: If you’re a quant, you know non-linear VaR models, you run volatility based MC portfolio simulations and the like.. Then you compute costs to determine how much to allocate to hedging versus maintaining long positions..or perhaps a volatility bet. Watching the levels and the data, you know which side to trim and by how much. A quant calculates all this, but it’s individual for everyone

comment.body: Listen, i’m telling you this as a fund manager. The market is at an all-time high. Nobody is buying here for the long term because we’re facing a lot of risks. We can see vol supply propping up the market, but a 5–10% correction would be healthy and timely. The problem isn’t an overvalued market, it’s illiquidity. There’s no investor confidence. The whole market is being held up by skew supply and the mag7. You can read that everywhere

comment.body:
I sent a screenshot yesterday where you can see that alma, on the exact bottom day, said there was vol selling and that she was taking long-delta trades. This was even above the paywall of the premarket post. She stressed it here and in the signals chat too. The reversion trades in april were brilliant and precise. She wrote about vol selling and the bessent put before anyone else did anywhere..including quite a few funds. 
And let’s not forget she called the march bull trap and said the downside target was the april 2024 low.. just one week before the april crash!!
I remember people here asking where alma posted it… It was in the Sunday post on March 31. Folks just didn’t read. There was a chart attached. How many went long there, I wonder? So yes, not reading and not paying attention is a big problem in this group too

comment.body: We never go long or short in big size until a move is confirmed. Only once the trend is proven do we cut the weight of hedges. This is how anyone who prioritizes risk minimization operates. The goal isn’t to be right or to say you called it in advance, it’s to preserve your capital and grow it steadily. And the slope of that growth should be as steep as possible while having the lowest possible risk
